source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 14.1-14.2 package concepts (pp. 255-256)

questions.q: What are the two ways to install software on Linux?
options: Install binary packages from a repository, or compile and install the source code package
Only compile from source
Only download binaries from a website
Copy the files manually or use FTP
questions.why: You either install pre-built packages from a repository (preferred - dependencies are handled) or compile the source code package yourself.

questions.q: Why does the guide recommend installing from the repository rather than compiling source?
options: The package management system resolves dependencies for you; resolving them by hand for a source build is hard
Compiling is impossible on Linux
Source packages contain viruses
Repositories are always faster to download
questions.why: The package manager solves dependency issues automatically, which is difficult to do manually when compiling source.

options: Source files pre-compiled into executable machine code and bundled into a binary file of a specific format
A single human-readable code file
A compressed archive of documentation
A repository configuration file
questions.why: In Linux, 'software package' means the binary package - source compiled into machine code and packaged in a defined format.

questions.q: What does RPM stand for, and which distributions use it?
options: RedHat Package Manager - used by Rocky Linux, Fedora, CentOS, SUSE and other Red Hat-family distros (.rpm files)
Rapid Package Method - used only by Debian
Remote Program Manager - used by all Linux
Red Package Model - used by Ubuntu
questions.why: RPM (RedHat Package Manager) is the Red Hat family's system, with .rpm packages; Debian and derivatives use DPKG and .deb.

questions.q: In the guide's terminology, what do 'RPM', 'rpm' and '.rpm' each refer to?
options: RPM = the package management system; rpm = the command; .rpm = the package file suffix
They are three names for the same command
RPM = the file; rpm = the system; .rpm = the command
All three refer to the repository
questions.why: Capital RPM is the system, lowercase rpm is the command, and .rpm is the file-format suffix.
